fields.description:
## Context

Agents frequently encounter friction during normal work — unclear instructions, missing guidance, repeated workarounds, tool timeouts — but have no channel to record these observations. Insights are lost when the session ends. The DSO plugin maintainer has no visibility into subjective pain points across sessions, and agents have no constructive action when they're stuck beyond escalating or giving up. By adding a lightweight suggestion capture mechanism with both agent-driven and automatic paths, friction patterns become observable, trackable, and actionable through retro synthesis.

## Success Criteria

- Agents can record a structured suggestion to .suggestions/ on the tickets orphan branch via suggestion-record.sh, with files named <timestamp>-<session-id>.json and a format that separates objective observations (what happened, metrics like wall-clock time and token counts) from subjective recommendations (what to change)
- A stop hook scans tool-error-counter.json and tool-use-*.jsonl at session end and records a mechanical-friction suggestion when configurable thresholds are exceeded (suggestion.error_threshold and suggestion.timeout_threshold in dso-config.conf, with conservative defaults derived from development environment data)
- Sub-agents emit SUGGESTION: <one-line summary> in their output text; a PostToolUse hook on Agent tool returns extracts the first such line per return, passes it to suggestion-record.sh, and logs a warning if the sentinel is malformed or absent
- using-lockpick guidance instructs agents to record suggestions when encountering repeated friction, unclear instructions, or workarounds — framing it as advisory guidance, not a hard threshold
- /dso:retro produces a Friction Suggestions section in its findings report: when .suggestions/ contains >= 1 record, clusters are ranked by frequency, each naming the affected guidance file, the observed friction pattern, and a proposed edit. When .suggestions/ is empty or absent, the section is omitted without error
- The ticket health script and ticket list do not include suggestion records

## Dependencies

No blocking epic dependencies. Assumes the following existing infrastructure is stable:
- tool-logging.sh and track-tool-errors.sh hooks (produce tool-use-*.jsonl and tool-error-counter.json)
- tickets orphan branch (initialized by /dso:onboarding)
- /dso:retro skill (consumer of .suggestions/)
- using-lockpick guidance file

## Approach

Unified write script + dual capture. Single suggestion-record.sh writes immutable JSON files to .suggestions/ on the tickets orphan branch. Agent-driven capture via using-lockpick guidance. Automatic capture via stop hook scanning existing tool logs. Sub-agent capture via SUGGESTION: sentinel extraction in PostToolUse hook. Retro synthesis clusters and proposes guidance edits. Permissive schema (few required fields, many optional).

## Scenario Analysis

- Concurrent writes to tickets orphan branch corrupt suggestion files (high, runtime): Multiple parallel agents writing suggestions simultaneously can cause git checkout conflicts or overlapping commits, losing suggestions silently
- Stop hook times out scanning large tool-use logs (medium, runtime): After long sprint sessions, log files grow large enough to exceed the hook timeout ceiling, killing the hook before it writes
- SUGGESTION: sentinel extraction captures partial content (medium, runtime): Multi-line sub-agent output may cause line-oriented extraction to truncate the payload
- tool-error-counter.json missing causes stop hook crash (high, runtime): Fresh environments or no-error sessions leave the file absent; unguarded reads crash the hook silently
- Suggestion files accumulate unboundedly (medium, runtime): No pruning/archival mechanism; retro performance degrades over hundreds of sessions
- tickets orphan branch missing .suggestions/ on first install (high, deployment): First invocation writes to a non-existent path; hook suppresses the error
- Existing installations without tickets branch break on upgrade (high, deployment): Projects without the orphan branch get silent failures from the write script
- CI environment lacks git identity (medium, deployment): Missing user.name/email causes commit failure in CI
- PostToolUse hook not registered on upgrade (high, deployment): Existing installations skip sentinel extraction entirely without error
- Threshold misconfiguration causes suggestion spam (medium, configuration): Too-low defaults flood retro with noise
- ticket list accidentally includes .suggestions/ files (medium, configuration): Path-based filters may miss edge cases
- Script path environment-dependent in worktrees (medium, configuration): Relative paths fail in worktree sessions
- Session ID collisions in parallel environments (medium, configuration): Low-entropy session IDs can cause file overwrites

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 13
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
